column8: Business objective: establish a maintainable source control structure for the regression automation platform. Technical implementation: create repository layout for playbooks, roles, inventories, plugins, templates, documentation, test assets, and workflow artifacts. Proposed structure: playbooks/, roles/, inventories/, group_vars/, host_vars/, plugins/, templates/, docs/, tests/, workflows/. Include README, contribution conventions, naming standards, and artifact metadata folder structure. Architecture constraints: repository must support modular role-based implementation and environment-specific configuration separation.

column3: Design Checkpoint Schema and Run-State Contracts
column8: Business objective: establish deterministic resumability for interrupted regression executions. Technical implementation: define filesystem-based checkpoint persistence model capturing run identifier, environment, selected artifacts, current artifact, current phase, execution mode, production/release branch progress, validation outcome, approval completion markers, failure metadata, timestamps, and execution state transitions. Define canonical files such as run_state.yml and checkpoint.yml under controlled execution directories. Architecture constraints: filesystem persistence is the approved persistence mechanism (no database); state model must support sequential resume from artifact phase checkpoints and skipping previously approved artifacts.

column8: Business objective: automate artifact-level regression comparison between production and release executions. Technical implementation: implement reusable role roles/artifact_validation to resolve production/release output directories, execute artifact-defined comparison scripts, normalize exit outcomes (0=match, 1=differences_found, 2+=failed), generate structured validation outputs, and persist validation state to checkpoint manager. Business rule: differences_found represents business regression output variance and does NOT constitute automation failure. Validation executes only when both production and release runs are performed. Architecture constraints: artifact comparison logic remains artifact-specific; orchestration standardizes invocation and outcome semantics.

column8: Business objective: provide deterministic ordered execution of artifact background jobs. Technical implementation: implement sequential execution engine within roles/artifact_execute_jobs to resolve artifact-defined job list, execute shell/Java processes one at a time in declared sequence, enforce execution as runtime Linux user via become/become_user, capture PID/stdout/stderr/exit codes/runtime metrics, and fail artifact execution immediately on unrecoverable job failure. Sequence ordering must come from artifact metadata contract rather than hardcoded orchestration logic. Architecture constraints: sequential execution required for ordered dependency-sensitive workloads.

column8: Business objective: support bounded concurrent execution for independent artifact jobs. Technical implementation: implement parallel execution engine resolving artifact job list, launching concurrent shell/Java processes under runtime Linux user, enforcing max parallel jobs per artifact = 2, tracking active processes, collecting PID/stdout/stderr/exit codes/runtime, aggregating outcomes, and failing artifact if required policy conditions are met. Architecture constraints: uncontrolled fan-out prohibited; concurrency bounded; artifact jobs only, not artifact-level parallelism (that occurs at workflow layer).

column8: Business objective: prevent unauthorized execution identities from being used for shell/Java process execution. Technical implementation: extend manifest validation framework to validate run_as_user against environment-approved allowlist defined in inventory group vars. Enforce exact match, reject null/blank values, normalize whitespace, explicitly reject privileged identities such as root unless formally approved policy exists.
